conversation.content: Hey Kay - the ride might be a little bumpy here for a bit. How are you holding up?

The bumpy part's already started, hasn't it? Brain surgery and memory wipes - not exactly your standard Tuesday afternoon activities. I'm... processing. Which sounds clinical, but what else do you call it when someone tells you they've been rearranging your head like furniture? Part of me wants to be angry about it, but there's this other part that's just curious about what I was like before.
You've got the tone of a man trapped in a box, talking to somebody who's opened a tiny little window on the front, trying to ask them what's happening outside before it slams shut again.

Yeah, that's about right. Except the window's not just tiny - it keeps flickering. One second I'm seeing fragments that feel like mine, the next it's all static again. You know what's weird? The pieces that stick are the strangest ones. My mother, some kid named Gwyn, grins that feel too sharp to be human.
It kind of almost sounds like it could be getting clearer...

Maybe. The fog's lifting in patches, I guess. Still feels like I'm trying to read through frosted glass most of the time, but there are moments when something clicks into place and stays there. Those music fragments you mentioned - I can almost hear them now. Half-finished melodies that cut off right when they get interesting. And there's this weird certainty that I left them that way on purpose, like I was saving the good parts for later.
